1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم ق ۚ والقرآن المجيد
1.english: Qaf. By the honored Qur'an...
1.transliteration: Qaaaf; wal Qur aanil Majeed

1.arabic: بل عجبوا أن جاءهم منذر منهم فقال الكافرون هذا شيء عجيب
1.english: But they wonder that there has come to them a warner from among themselves, and the disbelievers say, "This is an amazing thing.
1.transliteration: Bal 'ajibooo an jaa'ahum munzirum minhum faqaalal kaafiroona haazaa shai'un 'ajeeb

1.arabic: أإذا متنا وكنا ترابا ۖ ذلك رجع بعيد
1.english: When we have died and have become dust, [we will return to life]? That is a distant return."
1.transliteration: 'A-izaa mitnaa wa kunnaa turaaban zaalika raj'um ba'eed

1.arabic: قد علمنا ما تنقص الأرض منهم ۖ وعندنا كتاب حفيظ
1.english: We know what the earth diminishes of them, and with Us is a retaining record.
1.transliteration: Qad 'alimnaa maa tanqu-sul-ardu minhum wa 'indanaa Kitaabun Hafeez

1.arabic: بل كذبوا بالحق لما جاءهم فهم في أمر مريج
1.english: But they denied the truth when it came to them, so they are in a confused condition.
1.transliteration: Bal kazzaboo bilhaqqi lammaa jaaa'ahum fahum feee amrim mareej

1.arabic: أفلم ينظروا إلى السماء فوقهم كيف بنيناها وزيناها وما لها من فروج
1.english: Have they not looked at the heaven above them - how We structured it and adorned it and [how] it has no rifts?
1.transliteration: Afalam yanzurooo ilas samaaa'i fawqahum kaifa banainaahaa wa zaiyannaahaa wa maa lahaa min furooj

1.arabic: والأرض مددناها وألقينا فيها رواسي وأنبتنا فيها من كل زوج بهيج
1.english: And the earth - We spread it out and cast therein firmly set mountains and made grow therein [something] of every beautiful kind,
1.transliteration: Wal arda madadnaahaa wa alqainaa feehaa rawaasiya wa ambatnaa feehaa min kulli zawjim baheej

1.arabic: تبصرة وذكرى لكل عبد منيب
1.english: Giving insight and a reminder for every servant who turns [to Allah].
1.transliteration: Tabsiratanw wa zikraa likulli 'abdim muneeb

1.arabic: ونزلنا من السماء ماء مباركا فأنبتنا به جنات وحب الحصيد
1.english: And We have sent down blessed rain from the sky and made grow thereby gardens and grain from the harvest
1.transliteration: Wa nazzalnaa minas samaaa'i maaa'am mubaarakan fa ambatnaa bihee jannaatinw wa habbal haseed

1.arabic: والنخل باسقات لها طلع نضيد
1.english: And lofty palm trees having fruit arranged in layers -
1.transliteration: Wannakhla baasiqaatil laha tal'un nadeed

1.arabic: رزقا للعباد ۖ وأحيينا به بلدة ميتا ۚ كذلك الخروج
1.english: As provision for the servants, and We have given life thereby to a dead land. Thus is the resurrection.
1.transliteration: Rizqal lil'ibaad, wa ahyainaa bihee baldatam maitaa; kazaalikal khurooj

1.arabic: كذبت قبلهم قوم نوح وأصحاب الرس وثمود
1.english: The people of Noah denied before them, and the companions of the well and Thamud
1.transliteration: Kazzabat qablahum qawmu Noohinw wa Ashaabur Rassi wa Samood

1.arabic: وعاد وفرعون وإخوان لوط
1.english: And 'Aad and Pharaoh and the brothers of Lot
1.transliteration: Wa 'Aadunw wa Fir'awnu wikhwaanu loot

1.arabic: وأصحاب الأيكة وقوم تبع ۚ كل كذب الرسل فحق وعيد
1.english: And the companions of the thicket and the people of Tubba'. All denied the messengers, so My threat was justly fulfilled.
1.transliteration: Wa Ashaabul Aykati wa qawmu Tubba'; kullun kazzabar Rusula fahaqqa wa'eed

1.arabic: أفعيينا بالخلق الأول ۚ بل هم في لبس من خلق جديد
1.english: Did We fail in the first creation? But they are in confusion over a new creation.
1.transliteration: Afa'a yeenaa bilkhalqil awwal; bal hum fee labsim min khalqin jadeed

1.arabic: ولقد خلقنا الإنسان ونعلم ما توسوس به نفسه ۖ ونحن أقرب إليه من حبل الوريد
1.english: And We have already created man and know what his soul whispers to him, and We are closer to him than [his] jugular vein
1.transliteration: Wa laqad khalaqnal insaana wa na'lamu maa tuwaswisu bihee nafsuhoo wa Nahnu aqrabu ilaihi min hablil wareed

1.arabic: إذ يتلقى المتلقيان عن اليمين وعن الشمال قعيد
1.english: When the two receivers receive, seated on the right and on the left.
1.transliteration: 'Iz yatalaqqal mutalaqqi yaani 'anil yameeni wa 'anish shimaali qa'eed

1.arabic: ما يلفظ من قول إلا لديه رقيب عتيد
1.english: Man does not utter any word except that with him is an observer prepared [to record].
1.transliteration: Maa yalfizu min qawlin illaa ladaihi raqeebun 'ateed

1.arabic: وجاءت سكرة الموت بالحق ۖ ذلك ما كنت منه تحيد
1.english: And the intoxication of death will bring the truth; that is what you were trying to avoid.
1.transliteration: Wa jaaa'at kullu nafsim ma'ahaa saaa'iqunw wa shaheed

1.arabic: ونفخ في الصور ۚ ذلك يوم الوعيد
1.english: And the Horn will be blown. That is the Day of [carrying out] the threat.
1.transliteration: Wa nufikha fis Soor; zaalika yawmul wa'eed

1.arabic: وجاءت كل نفس معها سائق وشهيد
1.english: And every soul will come, with it a driver and a witness.
1.transliteration: Wa jaaa'at kullu nafsim ma'ahaa saaa'iqunw wa shaheed

1.arabic: لقد كنت في غفلة من هذا فكشفنا عنك غطاءك فبصرك اليوم حديد
1.english: [It will be said], "You were certainly in unmindfulness of this, and We have removed from you your cover, so your sight, this Day, is sharp."
1.transliteration: Laqad kunta fee ghaf latim min haazaa fakashafnaa 'anka ghitaaa'aka fabasarukal yawma hadeed

1.arabic: وقال قرينه هذا ما لدي عتيد
1.english: And his companion, [the angel], will say, "This [record] is what is with me, prepared."
1.transliteration: Wa qaala qareenuhoo haazaa maa ladaiya 'ateed

1.arabic: ألقيا في جهنم كل كفار عنيد
1.english: [Allah will say], "Throw into Hell every obstinate disbeliever,
1.transliteration: Alqiyaa fee Jahannama kulla kaffaarin 'aneed

1.arabic: مناع للخير معتد مريب
1.english: Preventer of good, aggressor, and doubter,
1.transliteration: Mannaa'il lilkhayri mu'tadim mureeb

1.arabic: الذي جعل مع الله إلها آخر فألقياه في العذاب الشديد
1.english: Who made [as equal] with Allah another deity; then throw him into the severe punishment."
1.transliteration: Allazee ja'ala ma'al laahi ilaahan aakhara fa alqiyaahu fil'azaabish shadeed

1.arabic: ۞ قال قرينه ربنا ما أطغيته ولكن كان في ضلال بعيد
1.english: His [devil] companion will say, "Our Lord, I did not make him transgress, but he [himself] was in extreme error."
1.transliteration: Qaala qareenuhoo Rabbanaa maaa atghaituhoo wa laakin kaana fee dalaahim ba'eed

1.arabic: قال لا تختصموا لدي وقد قدمت إليكم بالوعيد
1.english: [Allah] will say, "Do not dispute before Me, while I had already presented to you the warning.
1.transliteration: Qaala laa takhtasimoo ladaayya wa qad qaddamtu ilaikum bilwa'eed

1.arabic: ما يبدل القول لدي وما أنا بظلام للعبيد
1.english: The word will not be changed with Me, and never will I be unjust to the servants."
1.transliteration: Maa yubaddalul qawlu ladaiya wa maaa ana bizal laamil lil'abeed

1.arabic: يوم نقول لجهنم هل امتلأت وتقول هل من مزيد
1.english: On the Day We will say to Hell, "Have you been filled?" and it will say, "Are there some more,"
1.transliteration: Yawma naqoolu li'jahannama halim talaati wa taqoolu hal mim mazeed

1.arabic: وأزلفت الجنة للمتقين غير بعيد
1.english: And Paradise will be brought near to the righteous, not far,
1.transliteration: Wa uzlifatil jannatu lil muttaqeena ghaira ba'eed

1.arabic: هذا ما توعدون لكل أواب حفيظ
1.english: [It will be said], "This is what you were promised - for every returner [to Allah] and keeper [of His covenant]
1.transliteration: Haaza maa too'adoona likulli awwaabin hafeez

1.arabic: من خشي الرحمن بالغيب وجاء بقلب منيب
1.english: Who feared the Most Merciful unseen and came with a heart returning [in repentance].
1.transliteration: Man khashiyar Rahmaana bilghaibi wa jaaa'a biqalbim muneeb

1.arabic: ادخلوها بسلام ۖ ذلك يوم الخلود
1.english: Enter it in peace. This is the Day of Eternity."
1.transliteration: Udkhuloohaa bisalaamin zaalika yawmul khulood

1.arabic: لهم ما يشاءون فيها ولدينا مزيد
1.english: They will have whatever they wish therein, and with Us is more.
1.transliteration: Lahum maa yashaaa'oona feehaa wa ladainaa mazeed

1.arabic: وكم أهلكنا قبلهم من قرن هم أشد منهم بطشا فنقبوا في البلاد هل من محيص
1.english: And how many a generation before them did We destroy who were greater than them in [striking] power and had explored throughout the lands. Is there any place of escape?
1.transliteration: Wa kam ahlaknaa qablahum min qarnin hum ashaddu minhum batshan fanaqqaboo fil bilaad, hal mim mahees

1.arabic: إن في ذلك لذكرى لمن كان له قلب أو ألقى السمع وهو شهيد
1.english: Indeed in that is a reminder for whoever has a heart or who listens while he is present [in mind].
1.transliteration: Inna fee zaalika lazikraa liman kaana lahoo qalbun aw alqas sam'a wa huwa shaheed

1.arabic: ولقد خلقنا السماوات والأرض وما بينهما في ستة أيام وما مسنا من لغوب
1.english: And We did certainly create the heavens and earth and what is between them in six days, and there touched Us no weariness.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad khalaqnas samaawaati wal arda wa maa bainahumaa fee sittati ayyaamin wa maa massanaa mil lughoob

1.arabic: فاصبر على ما يقولون وسبح بحمد ربك قبل طلوع الشمس وقبل الغروب
1.english: So be patient, [O Muhammad], over what they say and exalt [Allah] with praise of your Lord before the rising of the sun and before its setting,
1.transliteration: Fasbir 'alaa maa yaqooloona wa sabbih bihamdi Rabbika qabla tuloo'ish shamsi wa qablal ghuroob

1.arabic: ومن الليل فسبحه وأدبار السجود
1.english: And [in part] of the night exalt Him and after prostration.
1.transliteration: Wa minal laili fasabbih hu wa adbaaras sujood

1.arabic: واستمع يوم يناد المناد من مكان قريب
1.english: And listen on the Day when the Caller will call out from a place that is near -
1.transliteration: Wastami' yawma yunaa dil munaadi mim makaanin qareeb

1.arabic: يوم يسمعون الصيحة بالحق ۚ ذلك يوم الخروج
1.english: The Day they will hear the blast [of the Horn] in truth. That is the Day of Emergence [from the graves].
1.transliteration: Yawma yasmaoonas sai hata bilhaqq zaalika yawmul khurooj

1.arabic: إنا نحن نحيي ونميت وإلينا المصير
1.english: Indeed, it is We who give life and cause death, and to Us is the destination
1.transliteration: Innaa Nahnu nuhyee wa numeetu wa ilainal maseer

1.arabic: يوم تشقق الأرض عنهم سراعا ۚ ذلك حشر علينا يسير
1.english: On the Day the earth breaks away from them [and they emerge] rapidly; that is a gathering easy for Us.
1.transliteration: Yawma tashaqqaqul ardu 'anhum siraa'aa; zaalika hashrun 'alainaa yaseer

1.arabic: نحن أعلم بما يقولون ۖ وما أنت عليهم بجبار ۖ فذكر بالقرآن من يخاف وعيد
1.english: We are most knowing of what they say, and you are not over them a tyrant. But remind by the Qur'an whoever fears My threat.
1.transliteration: Nahnu a'lamu bimaa yaqooloona wa maaa anta 'alihim bijabbaarin fazakkir bil quraani many yakhaafu wa'eed
